markets.question: Ross Barkley: Anytime Goalscorer
markets.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Burnley FC and Aston Villa FC, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 9:00 AM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ross Barkley is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". Own goals do not count. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "50-50". If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Player props center on Villa's attacking firepower led by Ollie Watkins amid their stronger recent form and head-to-head edge (wins in last two meetings, including 2-1 in October 2025), tempered by midfield absences: Amadou Onana (calf knock), Boubacar Kamara (knee), and John McGinn (hamstring). Burnley's defensive injuries to Jordan Beyer (hamstring) and Josh Cullen (knee), plus Connor Roberts' doubtful status (strain), curb threats from Zian Flemming and Lyle Foster, while their poor home record (2 wins in 17) heightens upset potential in this high-stakes finale.

Key absences shape sentiment: Cremonese striker Faris Moumbagna remains sidelined with an adductor injury since late March, while defender Federico Baschirotto exited training hurt on May 4; Pisa misses midfielder Marius Marin and winger Mattéo Tramoni. Federico Bonazzoli leads Cremonese scorers but faces Pisa's stout recent head-to-head record, winning the last three encounters, potentially limiting attacking output in this low-stakes survival scrap.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 2. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Weibo Gaming vs Ninjas in Pyjamas (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 10 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Weibo Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In LPL 2026 Split 2 Group Ascend, Weibo Gaming (5-8, 7th) faces Ninjas in Pyjamas (5-7, 6th) in a pivotal double round robin BO3 under Fearless Draft, where top-4 advance directly to playoffs bracket while bottom-4 drop to play-in. Both teams enter on two-game win streaks after WBG's 2-1 grind over Team WE and NIP's dominant 2-0 sweep of Anyone's Legend on May 7, boosting momentum amid tight standings decided by series wins, game differential, and head-to-head. NIP holds edge from 2-1 April victory, but WBG's recent macro improvements and jungle standout Jiejie could exploit NIP's occasional early-game lapses in this high-variance matchup with elimination risks looming.

markets.question: Will 50-74 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between April 27-May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from April 27, 2026, through May 3, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an overwhelming 98.3% implied probability for 25-49 ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz during the week of April 27 to May 3, 2026, driven by persistent US-Iran naval standoff and commercial shipping disruptions since late February. Real-time AIS data from trackers like Windward.ai confirm depressed volumes, with daily transits averaging 8-19 vessels recently—13 on April 27, similar on the 28th, and 5-6 thereafter—reflecting blockade enforcement, Iranian threats, and risk-averse rerouting amid soaring insurance premiums. This skin-in-the-game positioning underscores wisdom-of-crowds efficiency in pricing geopolitical risk. Resolution hinges on IMF Portwatch's official tally; a sudden diplomatic thaw or data revision could challenge this strong consensus, though no such catalysts have emerged.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chicago be between 62-63°F on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/il/chicago/KORD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Franco Colapinto be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Imperial Academy advanced undefeated from Group A in United21 Season 47 with a 2-0 record, dominating LPH Gaming 2-1 and Sangal ALTERS 2-0 via strong showings on Inferno, Dust II, and Ancient, fueling trader optimism on their playoff momentum despite a lower HLTV world ranking of #193. Higher-ranked Infinite (#59) exited Group D in second at 2-1, overcoming AaB esport twice in Bo3 deciders but stumbling 0-2 to Misa Esports on Dust II and Nuke, highlighting inconsistency on select maps. Infinite boasts superior recent form, including a United21 Season 46 grand final win over BIG Academy, backed by rifler sl3nd (1.27 rating) and AWPer mhN1. A last-minute stand-in (fnl replacing Blytz) for Infinite adds uncertainty to this Bo3 upper bracket quarterfinal.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Deutscher Wetterdienst (DWD) and ECMWF ensemble forecasts indicate a high near 16-17°C in Munich on May 11, driven by an approaching low-pressure system bringing increased cloud cover and shower risks after recent warm highs around 22°C on May 9. Model consensus shows the GFS slightly warmer at 18°C in drier scenarios, while ECMWF leans cooler with persistent overcast conditions limiting solar heating and surface temperatures. Trader sentiment reflects this spread, with 34.5% implied probability for 16°C versus 29.5% for 17°C, highlighting uncertainty in frontal boundary timing and precipitation amounts. New model runs overnight and DWD updates by morning could refine odds ahead of resolution based on official airport observations. Historical mid-May averages hover around 18°C, underscoring the transient cool-down.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the de facto leader of Venezuela at the end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who de facto holds and exercises the powers of the head of state of Venezuela on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, “de facto holds” refers to the individual who exercises primary executive authority over the Venezuelan state at that time, including effective control over the armed forces, national institutions, and core executive decision-making, regardless of formal title, constitutional designation, or international recognition.

Formal appointment, recognition by the United Nations, or recognition by foreign governments is not required.

If more than one individual claims to be head of state, this market will resolve to the individual who demonstrably exercises primary governing control within Venezuela’s territory at the specified time.

If no individual exercises effective governing control at the specified time, this market will resolve to “No Head of State”.

Indicators of de facto authority may include control over the armed forces and security services; control over executive ministries and state administrative institutions; enforcement of national laws; issuance of binding national directives; and effective control over the capital and core state infrastructure.

Symbolic status, foreign recognition without domestic control, nomination without effective authority, or expired prior service will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting assessing who exercises effective governing authority at the specified time.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a fragmented field for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize following the Norwegian Nobel Committee's April 30 disclosure of 287 secret nominees—208 individuals and 79 organizations—driving low implied probabilities across the board. Yulia Navalnaya edges as the slim frontrunner at 7.5% for carrying forward Alexei Navalny's anti-authoritarian campaign amid Russia's Ukraine invasion, while Donald Trump sits at 6.5% buoyed by public nominations from Cambodia, Israel, and Pakistan leaders citing his Abraham Accords legacy, and Volodymyr Zelenskyy at 5.5% for Ukraine's defiant resistance. Differentiators include Navalnaya's dissident narrative versus Trump's diplomatic dealmaking and Zelenskyy's wartime leadership, but the opaque committee process and history of upsets like 2025's Maria Corina Machado keep the market tightly contested ahead of the October announcement.

markets.question: Will Trump reduce the deficit before 2027?
markets.description:
The U.S. federal budget deficit for September 2025 was $197,949,630,362.16 (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) reports a lower monthly deficit in December 2026 than in September 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source will be the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) published by the U.S. Department of the Treasury (fiscaldata.treasury.gov). The month surplus can be found in the column labeled "Current Month Deficit Surplus Amount" in the the table "Summary of Receipts, Outlays, and Surplus or Deficit” in the MTS (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).  If no report is published by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET another credible source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 84.5% implied probability for "No," driven by Congressional Budget Office projections showing the fiscal year 2026 federal deficit at $1.9 trillion (5.8% of GDP), with year-to-date figures already exceeding $1 trillion through February amid persistent revenue shortfalls and spending pressures. President Trump's policy mix—extension of 2017 tax cuts adding $1.4 trillion to decade-long deficits, alongside 42% defense spending hikes to $1.5 trillion in the April FY2027 budget proposal—offsets proposed non-defense discretionary cuts, per CBO analysis. Recent tariff policy shifts could further widen shortfalls by $1.1 trillion over ten years, reinforcing fiscal expansion. Key catalysts include final FY2026 Treasury data by September and debt ceiling negotiations.

markets.question: Will US unemployment reach at least 5.0% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in an “Employment Situation Report” for a reference month in 2026 is greater than or equal to the listed percentage. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. This market may not resolve to “No” until the Employment Situation report for December 2026 is released. If no Employment Situation Report for December 2026 is released by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, however, this market will resolve based on all previously published data up to that time. 

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for each month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The April 2026 BLS report showed nonfarm payrolls rising 115,000—beating consensus estimates of 55,000—while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%, signaling labor market resilience despite household surveys pointing to shrinking employment and participation. Fed March projections peg 2026 unemployment at a median 4.4%, with CBO forecasting a 4.6% peak amid cooling job gains averaging 76,000 monthly YTD versus 2025's anemic 10,000. Polymarket trader consensus, backed by $380,000 in volume, prices modest deterioration risks tied to policy uncertainty and slowing growth. Watch the May jobs report on June 5 and June 16-17 FOMC for updated dot plots that could sway rate path expectations and hiring trends.

markets.question: Dota 2: PlayTime vs Yellow Submarine - Game 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Lower bracket quarterfinal 1 match between PlayTime and Yellow Submarine in the 1win Essence Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 10 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "PlayTime" if PlayTime win Game 1 against Yellow Submarine.

This market will resolve to "Yellow Submarine" if Yellow Submarine win Game 1 against PlayTime.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 1. 

If Game 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Dota 2: PlayTime vs Yellow Submarine (BO3) - 1win Essence Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Lower bracket quarterfinal 1 match between PlayTime and Yellow Submarine in the 1win Essence Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 10 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "PlayTime" if PlayTime win the match against Yellow Submarine.

This market will resolve to "Yellow Submarine" if Yellow Submarine win the match against PlayTime.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the 1win Essence I playoffs lower bracket quarterfinals, PlayTime faces elimination against Yellow Submarine in this pivotal BO3 after dropping from the upper bracket semifinals with a 1-2 loss to PARIVISION yesterday, seeking revenge from their 1-2 group stage defeat to Yellow Submarine on May 2. Yellow Submarine, undefeated at 4-0 in Group A, absorbed an early 0-2 upper quarterfinal setback to REKONIX before rallying with a 2-0 upset over Tundra Esports in lower round 1, showcasing 68% winrate over the past month amid strong showings against MOUZ and 1w Team. PlayTime rides mixed momentum from recent 2-0 and 2-1 victories over Nigma Galaxy and others but holds vulnerability against top foes, while stable rosters and current Dota 2 meta favor adaptable drafting in this high-stakes survival clash.

markets.question: Will Letitia James be arrested before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual is arrested by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being briefly detained but not arrested (e.g. such as in the South Korean judicial system when an individual is awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant)
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy, Team Spirit's academy squad with a February 2026 roster refresh featuring Kiryasoo, VILBy, and s1nside, entered Exort Series #25 Main Stage Round 1 BO3 against Wave Esports as the implied favorite based on organizational backing and prior head-to-head edges in Exort events. Wave, HLTV #251 with March roster additions of Katalic, sheezy, and Belof, showed competitive qualifier form including 2-0 wins over BIG Academy and LEO but struggled with consistency on Ancient and Dust2. Traders weighed roster cohesion in the Swiss format—where 3-0 advances to playoffs—pistol rounds, and map pools amid a stable CS2 meta, with no major patch shifts or withdrawals reported pre-match.

markets.question: Qingdao Hainiu FC vs. Dalian Yingbo FC: O/U 2.5
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Qingdao Hainiu FC and Dalian Yingbo FC combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dalian Yingbo's strong early-season form in the Chinese Super League, with nine points from five matches placing them second in the table, contrasts sharply with Qingdao Hainiu's struggles at 15th after a 1-0-3 record and minus-five goal difference, fueling trader consensus around the visitors' edge despite Hainiu's home advantage at Qingdao Tiantai Stadium on May 10. Recent results highlight Yingbo's resilience post heavy March defeats to Shanghai Shenhua and Wuhan Three Towns, while Hainiu's narrow win over Henan offers faint momentum amid relegation pressure. Unbeaten head-to-head history—1-1 draw and 2-0 Yingbo win in 2025—combined with no reported major injuries, underscores stylistic matchup dynamics and table positioning as pivotal for outcome probabilities.

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after June 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The summary for the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee meeting for June 2026 is scheduled to be released on June 18, 2026.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the Bank Rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of England's June 2026 meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of England (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If the Bank Rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of England's statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the start date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 94% implied probability to no change in the Bank of England base rate at its June 18, 2026, Monetary Policy Committee meeting, reflecting the MPC's 8-1 vote to hold rates steady at 3.75% on April 29 amid CPI inflation rising to 3.3% in March—driven by energy price pass-through from Middle East tensions—while core CPI eased slightly to 3.1% and unemployment held at 4.9%. This positioning underscores the Bank's cautious stance on persistent services inflation and wage dynamics, prioritizing data-dependent restraint over knee-jerk hikes. Realistic challenges include hotter-than-expected April CPI or May labor data signaling renewed price pressures, potentially reviving the minority view for a 25 basis point increase.

markets.question: Will Seattle have between 0.5 and 1 inches of precipitation in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in inches in Seattle between May 1 and May 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

The resolution source for this market will be NOAA, specifically the figure for May 2026 when the "Monthly summarized data" for "Seattle City Area" is selected with the variable set to "Precipitation" at the https://www.weather.gov/wrh/climate?wfo=sew link once that figure is finalized for the whole month of May 2026.

If the reported data falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 2 decimal places (e.g., 1.54), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Rangers, buoyed by a league-best +33 goal difference despite back-to-back losses to Hearts and Motherwell, bring aggressive transitions but face title-irrelevant pressure, heightening uncertainty in this must-win for second-placed Celtic chasing leaders Hearts.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

With an open-seat race ahead—no incumbent eligible—2026 outcomes will shape nominations, fundraising, and battleground state dynamics for frontrunners like JD Vance (GOP) and Gavin Newsom (Democrats).

eventMetadata.context_description: Senate Republicans, wielding a narrow majority, passed a FY2026 budget resolution on April 23 by a 50-48 vote, unlocking reconciliation instructions for a $72 billion spending package focused on DHS, ICE, and CBP funding amid partial government shutdown risks. Progress hinges on merging committee submissions, Byrd Rule compliance to avoid extraneous provisions, and securing 51 votes on the floor without filibuster. GOP divisions over deficits and policy add-ons, plus a compressed timeline, heighten uncertainty ahead of potential late-May votes.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the AL-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the AL-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Shomari Figures secured the Democratic nomination for Alabama's 2nd Congressional District unopposed ahead of the canceled May 19 primary, facing Republican Hampton Harris, who similarly advanced without opposition, setting up a clear general election matchup on November 3. Trader consensus prices Democrats at 67.5% due to the district's court-ordered boundaries—featuring 48.7% Black voting-age population—that delivered Figures' 2024 victory and earn Solid Democratic ratings from Cook Political Report and others. Recent GOP-led special session legislation (HB1, signed May 8) enables special primaries reverting to the 2023 map if federal courts lift injunctions, potentially dropping the district's Black voting-age population to 39.9% and favoring Republicans, but traders view court approval as unlikely given prior Voting Rights Act rulings and pending challenges.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner dominates trader consensus at 71.5% implied probability for the 2026 French Open title, fueled by his sweep of the Monte Carlo and Madrid Masters 1000 clay crowns in the past month—stunning Carlos Alcaraz in Monte Carlo's final before dismantling Alexander Zverev 6-1, 6-2 in Madrid—reclaiming world No. 1 status and boasting a 92 Clay Performance Score. Alcaraz's right wrist injury forced his Roland Garros withdrawal late April, while Novak Djokovic nurses a shoulder issue with scant clay match wins, elevating Zverev (6.6%) as next via his Madrid final run and strong surface history. French hopefuls Arthur Fils and Lorenzo Musetti trail amid Sinner's top-seed path, though upsets loom in the five-set Grand Slam format on slow red clay.

markets.question: Will Under Armour (UAA) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Under Armour is estimated to release earnings on May 12, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Under Armour’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-0.02 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Under Armour reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $-0.02 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Under Armour releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

markets.question: Will Germany send warships through the Strait of Hormuz by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a national government, its military, or a broad consensus of credible reporting confirms that the listed country's warships transited through the Strait of Hormuz between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A "warship transit" is defined as a military vessel passing through the Strait of Hormuz. Military cargo or support vessels will be considered “warships”; however, commercial or civilian vessels will not qualify.

For the purposes of this market, only transits through the Strait of Hormuz will be considered, defined as passage through the narrowest portion of the waterway between Iran and Oman. Operations solely in the Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, or Arabian Sea without passage through this narrowest section will not qualify.

Official confirmation by a national government or its military that its vessels transited through the Strait of Hormuz during the specified timeframe will resolve this market immediately. An overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming that such a transit occurred during the specified timeframe will also suffice.

Qualifying confirmations include statements such as official announcements that a country has deployed naval vessels to transit or escort shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

Confirmations referring only to naval presence in the broader region, including the Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, or Arabian Sea, without confirmed transit through the Strait itself, as well as aerial operations, cyber operations, or actions by proxies or third parties, will not alone qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information by the respective national governments or their militaries; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the Strait of Hormuz warship transit market reflects heightened geopolitical risk premiums embedded in Brent crude prices, trading around $100 per barrel as of May 10, 2026—up nearly 50% from pre-crisis levels amid Iran's blockade since February that throttles 20% of global seaborne oil flows. U.S. Navy destroyers, including USS Truxtun, USS Mason, USS Rafael Peralta, USS Frank E. Peterson, and USS Michael Murphy, have executed multiple transits in early May under Project Freedom, intercepting Iranian missiles and drones while denying hits, cementing strong consensus on American involvement. The UK's HMS Dragon deployment toward the region, alongside France and Italy's positioning for a potential multinational defensive mission, signals expanding coalition dynamics, with tanker freight rates volatile ahead of May 31 resolution and IRGC submarine threats or diplomatic breakthroughs.

markets.question: Israel x Hezbollah diplomatic meeting by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding Israel-Hezbollah relations on behalf of their governments/leadership structures. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the government of Israel and Hezbollah leadership, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-brokered direct talks between Israel and Lebanon, the first since 1993, commenced in Washington on April 14, 2026, emphasizing Hezbollah disarmament, ceasefire extensions, and border security amid ongoing hostilities. Hezbollah has firmly rejected direct negotiations with Israel, excluding itself from the discussions, though its leader Naim Qassem signaled limited openness to indirect diplomacy on May 4. A fragile ceasefire, initiated April 16 and repeatedly extended, faces violations including Israel's first post-ceasefire strikes on Beirut around May 6-8. Traders eye the third round of Israel-Lebanon talks set for May 14-15, which may include military representatives for the first time, but Hezbollah's non-state status and opposition dim prospects for any direct Israel-Hezbollah diplomatic meeting.

markets.question: Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Saudi Professional League game between Al Shabab Saudi Club and Al Ittihad Saudi Club, scheduled for April 10 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Al Shabab Saudi Club and Al Ittihad Saudi Club each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on slstat.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Saudi Professional League game, scheduled for April 10 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Al Ittihad sit sixth in the Saudi Pro League table with 42 points from 26 matches, but their recent form has faltered with just one win in the last five games (1W-2D-2L), exacerbated by the winter departures of stars Karim Benzema to Al Hilal and N'Golo Kanté, leaving gaps in attack and midfield amid fan frustration ahead of a King's Cup semi-final. Al Shabab, 12th on 29 points, host at SHG Arena on April 10 showing sharper scoring edge lately (three wins in five, 12 goals), buoyed by home strength despite mid-table position and forward Mohammed Harbush sidelined long-term with a cruciate injury. Al Ittihad hold a strong head-to-head edge, unbeaten in the last five meetings, setting up a competitive Riyadh clash influenced by rest advantages and tactical adjustments.

markets.question: Will the price of XRP be between $0.90 and $1.00 on May 11?
markets.description:

markets.question: Will David Njoku play for Indianapolis Colts in 2026-27?
markets.description:

If David Njoku does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Free agent tight end David Njoku remains unsigned more than six weeks into the 2026 NFL offseason, driving trader consensus to spread implied probabilities evenly across multiple TE-needy contenders like the Falcons, Bengals, Eagles, and Chargers, all clustered near 48% amid their salary cap space, quarterback upgrades, and schemes favoring pass-catching tight ends. His public announcement ruling out a Cleveland Browns return—coupled with the team's draft investments in younger tight ends like Harold Fannin—has sunk the Browns to 24.5%, redirecting bets to rivals. Recent free agent visits to the Chargers and Ravens have nudged those odds higher without crowning a leader, underscoring the fluid free agency dynamics and Njoku's full recovery from 2025 knee issues keeping the race tightly contested.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick (R) holds a commanding fundraising lead with over $7.6 million cash on hand entering the May 19 Democratic primary, where Bucks County Commissioner Bob Harvie leads Lucia Simonelli as the likely challenger in this D+1 battleground district pivotal to Democrats' House majority flip path amid Republicans' slim 218-214 edge. Trader consensus tilts slightly Democratic at 52.5% despite a March poll showing Fitzpatrick ahead 48-42 over Harvie, reflecting Fitzpatrick's recent bipartisan votes—including support for Haitian TPS extension on April 16 and Canada tariff repeal—that have drawn GOP primary ire and depressed base enthusiasm. Separation could arise from the primary outcome, national midterm headwinds, or heavy outside spending targeting Bucks County's swing voters.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls, including Ideia (May 1-5) showing President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva at 40% and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro at 36% in stimulated first-round scenarios, alongside Real Time Big Data (May 2-4) with Lula at 40% and Flávio at 34%, position Flávio as the clear runner-up for Brazil's October 4 presidential first round. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects this polling trend, where Flávio Bolsonaro commands 66.5% implied probability for second place, far ahead of Lula (19.5%)—whom surveys consistently project to lead—and lower shares for rivals like Ronaldo Caiado (5-6%), Romeu Zema (3-4%), and Renan Santos (up to 5%). Flávio's recent Senate leadership in blocking Lula's Supreme Court nominee has further solidified his opposition credentials among conservatives, narrowing the gap and boosting his path to a likely runoff.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 62.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 63 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

events.title: Dota 2: Nigma Galaxy vs REKONIX (BO3) - 1win Essence Group B
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Nigma Galaxy" if Nigma Galaxy win the match against REKONIX.

This market will resolve to "REKONIX" if REKONIX win the match against Nigma Galaxy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nigma Galaxy entered their BO3 matchup against REKONIX in 1win Essence I Group B as undefeated frontrunners, riding a four-win streak in recent Tier 2 events and holding a top-20 world ranking with veteran execution in drafts and teamfights. REKONIX, an Indonesian upstart with a 2-2 group record prior, showed flashes in upsets like their 2-1 over Team Nemesis but struggled against elite macro play. No roster changes, player absences, or patch shifts disrupted the meta, emphasizing Nigma's home-region edge in online play and head-to-head stylistic matchup favoring their flexible hero pool. The series on May 2 highlighted group seeding stakes, with Nigma's momentum securing playoff positioning amid closely contested Tier 2 brackets.

eventMetadata.context_description: In LPL Split 2 Group Ascend double round-robin, Anyone's Legend and JD Gaming sit tied at 7-4, battling for a top-4 direct playoffs spot amid Fearless Draft BO3s, with trader consensus pricing AL around 68% implied probability due to superior map differential (+5 vs. JDG's +4) and Tarzan's jungle impact despite occasional laning inconsistencies. eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs hold a 2-1 series lead over the Minnesota Timberwolves in their Western Conference semifinals matchup, with Game 4 set for today at Target Center amid Minnesota's home-court advantage. Spurs dominated Game 2 with a 38-point rout—the Wolves' worst postseason loss—and followed with a 115-108 victory in Game 3 on the road, showcasing superior depth and defense despite splitting the regular-season series 1-2. Minnesota's trader sentiment hinges on injury concerns: Donte DiVincenzo remains out with an Achilles tear, while Anthony Edwards (left knee bone bruise) and Ayo Dosunmu (right heel soreness) are questionable after Edwards' midseries return sparked Game 1's upset win. Spurs enter fully healthy, leveraging their 62-20 regular-season record and rest advantages.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

High-resolution models like the High-Resolution Rapid Refresh (HRRR) and Global Forecast System (GFS) align on minimums near 50.5°F amid lingering cloud cover from recent showers and light northerly winds, which limit radiational cooling compared to clearer analogs. Early morning observations already bottomed near 52°F, reinforcing stability. Realistic challenges include unexpected sky clearing for enhanced cooling into the upper 40s or denser fog trapping heat above 52°F, though model ensembles show low confidence in deviations ahead of final NWS updates.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Darth Vader" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects razor-thin uncertainty for San Francisco's highest temperature on May 12, with 56-57°F (50%) edging out 47°F or below (49.5%), driven by divergent guidance on marine layer persistence amid steady onshore flow. The National Weather Service's latest marine stratus forecast notes stratus returning overnight into May 10, with coastal ceilings holding through May 11 and incomplete burn-off due to compressed layer depths under inversion, capping diurnal heating and favoring cooler outcomes; slight cooling is projected for May 12 as winds sustain the cool California Current influence. Key differentiators include layer thickness—thicker decks (>1,500 ft) suppress highs below 50°F via stratus shading, while earlier dissipation or subsidence boosts them to upper 50s. Watch 12Z GFS/ECMWF runs and NWS updates for refined model ensembles.

markets.question: Another US debt downgrade before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States' long-term sovereign credit letter rating is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point by December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Another US debt downgrade before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States' long-term sovereign credit letter rating is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point by December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 77% implied probability against another US sovereign debt downgrade before 2027, driven by rating agencies' stable outlooks amid robust GDP growth offsetting fiscal pressures. Fitch's April 30 report affirmed AA+ stable despite projecting general government deficits near 7.9% of GDP in 2026-27 and debt-to-GDP surpassing 120% by end-2027, echoing Moody's Aa1 stable (post-2025 downgrade) and S&P's AA+ stable. The July 2025 debt ceiling increase to $41.1 trillion leaves headroom with debt at ~$39 trillion and no recent political gridlock. Key catalysts include FY2026 deficit realizations—projected at $1.9 trillion by CBO—and potential 2027 ceiling renewal, where sustained economic resilience could preserve ratings.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors a high temperature of 68°F or higher in Denver on May 11, reflecting strong alignment across National Weather Service forecasts and major models like GFS and European ECMWF, projecting daytime highs of 75–87°F under clear skies. This positioning stems from a potent high-pressure ridge building over the central Rockies following a major spring snowstorm earlier this week that dumped up to half a foot in the metro area, now giving way to post-frontal warming, light winds, and classic downslope enhancement east of the Front Range. Such patterns typically yield above-normal May temperatures, with climatological averages around 70°F. Realistic challenges include an unexpected surge of northerly flow or persistent low clouds delaying solar heating, though model runs show minimal disagreement; watch NWS updates through midday May 10 for final refinements.

markets.question: Will Google have the #2 AI model at the end of June 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

eventMetadata.context_description: Somaliland's House of Elders (Guurti) approved a 27-month term extension for the House of Representatives and local councils on April 28, 2026, postponing parliamentary and local elections originally scheduled for May 2026 and pushing them beyond 2027, which has driven trader consensus to price "No election before 2027" at 72%. The National Electoral Commission had sought only a 10-month delay for voter registration and logistics, but the Guurti's longer extension—despite criticism from opposition parties like KAAH and civil society over democratic erosion—reflects recurring delays since the last parliamentary vote in 2005. Among parties, Waddani leads at 31.6% on momentum from President Abdirahman Irro's 2024 presidential win, ahead of Justice and Welfare (UCID) at 5.1% and Kulmiye at 1.6%, amid uncertain timelines and potential legal challenges.

eventMetadata.context_description: Switzerland's commanding 76.5% implied probability in this FIFA World Cup 2026 Group B matchup stems from their superior FIFA ranking (19th) versus Qatar's 57th, bolstered by a talent pool from top European leagues and consistent knockout-stage appearances in recent tournaments, contrasting Qatar's group-stage exit without points at the 2022 World Cup. Neutral-site play at Levi's Stadium levels the field slightly, pricing a draw at 15.5% amid Qatar's defensive resilience and lone 1-0 friendly win over Switzerland in 2018, while Qatar's 8.8% reflects their underdog status despite Asian qualifier success. No major injuries or lineup changes emerged in the past 30 days, with both sides tuning up via friendlies like Switzerland's upcoming clash against Jordan.

markets.question: Will Rocket Lab USA, Inc. (RKLB) hit (LOW) $76 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Rocket Lab USA, Inc. (RKLB) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Rocket Lab USA, Inc. (RKLB) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.RKLB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.RKLB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' single-lap dominance, securing all poles through Japan with Kimi Antonelli and George Russell, has propelled them to the constructors' lead at 135 points after Miami, yet trader consensus prices every team near even at 49.5% for Canadian Grand Prix constructor pole position amid the 2026 regs' parity push. Circuit Gilles Villeneuve's razor-thin margins, punishing kerbs, and Wall of Champions chicane amplify unpredictability, favoring clean laps over raw pace as track evolution and clean air prove decisive. Ferrari trails closely at 90 points with strong Q3 threats from Leclerc and Hamilton, while midfield risers like Haas and Alpine eye upgrades; newcomers Audi Revolut and Cadillac lag but could surprise in low-downforce setup.

markets.question: Will Hong Kong have between 230-240mm of precipitation in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in mm in Hong Kong between May 1 and May 31, 2026, according to the Hong Kong Observatory.

The resolution source for this market will be the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Total Rainfall (mm)" figure for all days in May 2026 under the "Daily Extract" at the https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm link once figures are finalized for the specified range.

If the reported data falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

If the relevant data is not available by July 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 1 decimal place (e.g., 1.5), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
