eventMetadata.context_description: Gold futures for the June 2026 contract (GC) trade around $4,724 per ounce, reflecting trader consensus on sustained upside amid de-escalating US-Iran tensions that have eased oil-driven inflation premia and revived expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts. Optimism over a ceasefire in the Strait of Hormuz, confirmed by President Trump, has weakened the US dollar and fueled a weekly gain exceeding 2%, countering pressures from March 2026 CPI's 1.1% monthly surge and a robust April nonfarm payrolls beat of 115,000 jobs versus 62,000 anticipated. Central bank gold reserves, including China's 18th straight monthly addition, provide structural support. Key catalysts ahead include April CPI on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, where persistent inflation above 3% could cap gains near $5,000 while softer data unlocks further monetary easing.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Taipei be 33°C or higher on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Taipei Songshan Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Taipei Songshan Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tw/taipei/RCSS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Central Weather Administration (CWA) observations from Taipei Songshan Airport station—the official metric for this market—confirm the highest temperature on May 10, 2026, peaked at 29°C mid-afternoon amid partly cloudy skies and moderate humidity around 70-80%, anchoring trader consensus at 100% implied probability for this outcome.** This reading matches Taipei's climatological average daily high for May of approximately 29°C (84°F), following cooler recent days like May 9's 22°C maximum under persistent cloud cover. Real-time hourly data shows temperatures declining into the evening with light southerly winds, minimizing escalation risk. While an improbable late-day solar heating surge or data adjustment could challenge this, current meteorological trends and proximity to daily close render higher outcomes negligible, with final CWA summary pending resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona top the La Liga table with a commanding lead over second-placed Real Madrid ahead of their pivotal El Clásico on May 10 at Spotify Camp Nou, where high-stakes title implications fuel aggressive play. Both sides generate substantial corners—Barcelona averaging 7.35 at home with matches totaling 11.27, Real Madrid 6.13 away amid 10.18 overall—reflecting possession-dominant styles and frequent wide attacks under Hansi Flick and Carlo Ancelotti. Past Clásicos average over 11 corners, underscoring rivalry-driven intensity.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat April McClain Delaney leads trader consensus at 87.5% implied probability to win Maryland's 6th Congressional District House seat, reflecting the district's Safe Democratic rating from Sabato's Crystal Ball and Cook Political Report amid a partisan lean favoring Democrats by wide margins in recent cycles. A Public Sentiment Institute poll from April 8-11 showed Delaney at 55% against a generic Republican at 31%, with challenger David Trone polling even stronger at 59%, underscoring Democratic dominance regardless of the June 23 primary outcome between the heavily self-funded rivals. Weak Republican fundraising—top GOP candidates under $310,000 raised—and historical incumbency advantages in this suburban-rural district sustain low odds for Republicans at 9%, though national midterm dynamics could introduce volatility ahead of November 3.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Consumer Price Index data revealed a sharp reacceleration to 3.3% year-over-year inflation in March 2026, up from 2.4% in February, driven primarily by a 10.9% energy price surge amid Middle East supply disruptions. The Federal Reserve's preferred core PCE gauge climbed to 3.2% in March, prompting the March FOMC dot plot to revise year-end 2026 PCE projections upward to 2.7% from 2.4%. Cleveland Fed nowcasts signal headline CPI near 3.9% for May, reflecting trader consensus on sticky pressures from commodities and robust demand. Traders eye the April CPI release on May 12 as pivotal, alongside the June 17-18 FOMC meeting, where rate cut expectations could pivot on inflation trajectory versus the 2% target.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol remains in detention following his February 2026 life imprisonment sentence for leading an insurrection tied to his 2024 martial law declaration, after the Constitutional Court upheld his impeachment and removal from office in April 2025. Multiple convictions, including a five-year term for obstructing arrest (upheld and increased to seven years by an appeals court on April 29, 2026), reinforce trader consensus implying a 92.1% probability he stays in custody through 2026. Ongoing appeals in his insurrection trial, with hearings as recent as May 7, show no bail grants or release signals, amid procedural hurdles like extended detention warrants that historically prolong high-profile cases. Late-breaking acquittals or health-related pardons could shift odds, though none appear imminent.

markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $40 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil June 2026 futures have pulled back to around $96 per barrel after spiking above $105 in early May, as traders unwound the geopolitical risk premium tied to Strait of Hormuz disruptions amid easing tensions and U.S. intervention signals from President Trump. The latest EIA data showed a 2.3 million barrel inventory draw for the week ended May 1, leaving stocks at 457.2 million barrels—1% above the five-year seasonal average—while refinery runs dipped slightly. OPEC+8 approved a modest 206,000 barrels per day production quota increase for May, signaling gradual supply normalization. Traders eye weekly EIA releases, including May 13, Northern Hemisphere summer driving demand, and any Hormuz escalation for near-term price swings ahead of June settlement.

markets.question: Will Arsenal score the most goals in 2025-26 UEFA Champions League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the club that records the most total goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Champions League competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Champions League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the club who advanced farther in the competition. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the club that is listed first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Champions League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/statistics/clubs/goals/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain holds a commanding lead in UEFA Champions League total goals scored after powering through the semi-finals with a 6-4 aggregate victory featuring Ousmane Dembélé's crucial strike, cementing trader consensus at 99.5% implied probability for PSG as the competition's top-scoring club ahead of the final against Arsenal. PSG's explosive Ligue phase output and knockout dominance, bolstered by Khvicha Kvaratskhelia's record goal involvements, have built an insurmountable buffer over Arsenal's tally, despite the Gunners' solid campaign led by Viktor Gyökeres. Arsenal could challenge with a lopsided final win—scoring multiple without reply—but PSG's attacking depth and favoritism make this unlikely, reflecting the wisdom of crowds in real-money wagers.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Amsterdam be 15°C on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Amsterdam Airport Schiphol Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Amsterdam Airport Schiphol Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/nl/schiphol/EHAM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors 16°C as Amsterdam's highest temperature today, with 98% implied probability, driven by the latest KNMI and ECMWF ensemble forecasts projecting a peak around 16°C at Amsterdam Airport Schiphol—the market's official resolution station. Midday observations logged 14.7°C under light cloud cover and northerly winds (NO 3), consistent with a cool maritime air mass limiting daytime heating despite typical May climatology averaging 16–17°C highs. Model agreement reflects persistent overcast conditions capping solar insolation, with minimal spread in the 16–18°C range. Realistic challenges include unexpected cloud breaks allowing greater insolation or a late-afternoon wind shift, though hourly KNMI updates through evening will refine this as the market nears resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in forecast models for Amsterdam's highest temperature on May 12, with KNMI guidance pointing to a maximum around 14°C amid light showers alternating with sun, westerly winds at force 4, and a chilly overnight low near 5°C. Ensemble means from ECMWF and GFS cluster near 12°C, but significant spread—driven by variable cloud cover, boundary layer mixing, and timing of potential precipitation—differentiates outcomes like 11–13°C, all trading near 20%. Cooler-than-average May conditions stem from persistent maritime airflow from the North Sea, capping warm-up potential. Watch for 00Z model updates overnight and KNMI refinements tomorrow, as small shifts in insolation could tip the balance.

markets.question: Will the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting be on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed date (Pakistan Standard Time) on which the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran occurs.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

If no qualifying meeting occurs by May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM Pakistan Standard Time, this market will resolve to “No Meeting before May 11”.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors no US-Iran diplomatic meeting before May 11 at 98.6%, driven by the absence of any announced direct engagement on May 10 amid escalating tensions in the ongoing 2026 Iran war. Iran transmitted its response to a US peace proposal via Pakistani mediators today, focusing on ending hostilities and reopening the Strait of Hormuz, but these remain indirect negotiations following a week of traded attacks, including US strikes on Iranian targets after reported firings on American warships. Prior face-to-face talks in Islamabad stalled without agreement, underscoring persistent hurdles over nuclear issues and sanctions. A commanding lead reflects low expectations for an abrupt breakthrough; only a surprise official announcement of direct talks could shift odds before resolution.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NM-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NM-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Melanie Stansbury commands trader consensus at 90.5% implied probability to win New Mexico's 1st Congressional District House seat, driven by the urban Albuquerque-based district's strong Democratic lean (historical D+8 margins) and her proven incumbency advantage after wide victories in prior cycles. Recent FEC filings highlight Stansbury's dominant fundraising edge over an uncompetitive Republican primary field ahead of the June 2 primaries, with no high-profile GOP challengers emerging to date. This safe Democratic stronghold shows no notable shifts in the past 30 days, underscoring limited paths to an upset. Realistic challenges include a scandal hitting Stansbury, a surprise GOP recruit gaining national backing, or broader midterm turnout surges favoring Republicans, though structural barriers persist through the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% in its April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting—the third consecutive pause—citing persistent inflation and a resilient labor market despite economic uncertainty. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year, the highest since May 2024, with core measures remaining sticky and PCE inflation nearing 3.5%, prompting traders to reassess the pace of potential rate cuts. CME Fed funds futures currently price in modest easing expectations over the next 12 months, reflecting trader consensus on gradual monetary policy normalization. Key catalysts include April CPI data (due mid-May), nonfarm payrolls, and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, where dot plot updates could shift the market-implied rate path through 2026.

markets.question: Will Erling Haaland be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025-26 EPL season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No". 

Goals scored in Premier League matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., FA Cup, Carabao Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market. 

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Erling Haaland commands a dominant 97.4% implied probability as Premier League top goalscorer, fueled by his league-leading 26 goals after 34 matchdays, a four-goal cushion over Brentford's Igor Thiago at 22. Manchester City's title-chasing momentum and softer remaining fixtures—such as hosting Crystal Palace midweek—bolster trader consensus, while Thiago's side faces tougher tests amid mid-table security. Haaland's elite finishing (25.2 xG) and eight assists underscore his consistency, with no recent injury concerns. Upsets remain possible via Haaland absence from rotation or injury, or Thiago netting multiple hauls in Brentford's final four games, though historical Golden Boot chases rarely see such catch-ups this late.

markets.question: Will Meituan have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 has solidified trader consensus at 95.5% implied probability for the best coding AI model by end of May, driven by its commanding leads across key benchmarks like SWE-bench Verified (over 90% resolution rates) and LMSYS Code Arena, where it outpaces rivals by 40+ points in agentic coding tasks such as building live web apps. Released in mid-April, the model excels in multi-file reasoning, bug fixing, and real-world software engineering, per official evaluations and third-party leaderboards, outshining OpenAI's GPT-5.4 and Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro. While uncertainty persists—potential GPT-5.5 launches or Google I/O surprises could narrow the gap—Anthropic's multi-model dominance (multiple Claudes in top ranks) and lack of recent challenger breakthroughs underpin the strong positioning, with resolution hinging on late-May updates.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's "What will be said on ICEMAN?" market heavily favors "Daddy" at 81% and "Covid" at 80% implied probability, driven by Drake's signature autobiographical style spotlighting fatherhood themes around son Adonis and pandemic-era callbacks from his Toronto lockdown reflections. These lead amid a cryptic rollout featuring massive ice block stunts revealing the May 15 release date via OVO Sound and Republic Records—his first solo album since 2023's For All the Dogs—plus "Iceman Episode" video teasers and Adin Ross streams hinting at personal narratives over direct beef jabs, as seen in lower odds for "Kendrick/Lamar" (26%). With Episode 4 livestream tomorrow (May 14) and full streaming debut locking transcripts for resolution on Spotify/Apple Music, last-minute hype could shift positioning before charts gauge first-week dominance against minimal May competition.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Aubry Bracco at an implied 87% probability to win Survivor 50, solidified by her masterful gameplay through the final 7 after last week's shocking double boot episode eliminated Ozzy Lusth and Emily Flippen. markets.question: Map Handicap: GL (-1.5) vs Sinners (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 2 match between Sinners and GamerLegion in the IEM Atlanta Group B, initially scheduled for May 11 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "GamerLegion" if GamerLegion wins 2 or more maps than Sinners in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Sinners". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Sinners vs GamerLegion (BO3) - IEM Atlanta Group B
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 2 match between Sinners and GamerLegion in the IEM Atlanta Group B, initially scheduled for May 11 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Sinners" if Sinners win the match against GamerLegion.

This market will resolve to "GamerLegion" if GamerLegion win the match against Sinners.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: GamerLegion enters as slight trader favorite over SINNERS in their IEM Atlanta 2026 Group B BO3 opener, buoyed by a 2-1 head-to-head edge including a 2-0 win in February's Parken Challenger Championship on Inferno and Nuke. Holding HLTV #31 ranking to SINNERS' #32, GamerLegion boasts a 70% win rate over the past three months, highlighted by a recent 2-1 upset over FURIA in BLAST Rivals last week before a 0-2 loss to top-ranked Natus Vincere on May 1. SINNERS rides a three-match win streak from LORGAR Rankings against lower-tier foes like Gentle Mates and KOLESIE in late April, with strong Nuke (80% win rate) and Mirage (67%) stats mirroring GamerLegion's strengths. Stable rosters for both—GamerLegion's REZ, Tauson, hypex, PR, Snax; SINNERS' SHOCK, MoDo, beastik, kisserek, stressarN—face a high-stakes double-elimination group alongside NAVI, Liquid, and Astralis, where map vetoes on the active pool including Dust2, Overpass, Ancient, and Anubis could prove decisive amid no major roster disruptions.

Rodrigo Pacheco (PSB) trails at 18.5% market odds despite slipping to 6% in the latest poll, while Gabriel Azevedo (MDB) at 13.6% and others lag, highlighting opposition consolidation risks ahead of the first-round vote.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Hakeem Jeffries' commanding position as House Minority Leader in the solidly Democratic NY-08 district, rated Solid D by Cook Political Report with a D+26 partisan lean, drives trader consensus heavily favoring the Democratic Party at 92% for the November 3 general election. Jeffries dominates the June 23 Democratic primary market at 96% against challenger Vance Bostic, reflecting strong fundraising and establishment support amid minimal recent primary turbulence. No prominent Republican nominee has emerged, underscoring historical lopsided margins exceeding 70%. Scenarios like a primary upset yielding a flawed nominee, Jeffries scandal, or national Republican wave could shift odds, though structural advantages make an upset unlikely.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Royal Challengers Bangalore vs Mumbai Indians - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Royal Challengers Bangalore and Mumbai Indians scheduled for 2026-05-10 in Indian Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Indian Premier League: Royal Challengers Bangalore vs Mumbai Indians
events.description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Royal Challengers Bangalore and Mumbai Indians scheduled for May 10 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Both Mumbai Indians and Royal Challengers Bengaluru enter their IPL 2026 Match 20 at Wankhede Stadium desperate for a bounce-back after defeats to Rajasthan Royals—MI's rain-curtailed 27-run loss and RCB's six-wicket reverse—highlighting MI's leaky bowling economy beyond Jasprit Bumrah and RCB's middle-overs stutters despite batting depth. RCB sit third in the points table with stronger recent form, while MI battle a slow start at ninth, bolstered by Mitchell Santner's return from a shoulder niggle and Trent Boult's powerplay threat. Wankhede's batsman-friendly Pitch No. 8 favors dew-assisted chases (run rate 10.10 second innings), with spin more economical; toss winner likely bowls first. Key matchups include Virat Kohli versus Bumrah and Rohit Sharma's strike rate quest, amplifying this high-stakes rivalry where MI hold a 21-14 head-to-head edge.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?

markets.question: Nvidia Data Center Revenue above 50B in Q1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Nvidia’s data center revenue for the first fiscal quarter of 2027, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.
The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released, and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”. 
 
If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by July 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market. 

The resolution source for this market is Nvidia’s official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects strong confidence in NVIDIA's data center revenue exceeding key thresholds for fiscal Q1 2027 (ending April 2026), driven by the segment's explosive 75% year-over-year growth to a record $62.3 billion in Q4 FY2026, which accounted for 91% of total $68.1 billion revenue. Management's $78 billion (±2%) total revenue guidance—explicitly excluding China data center compute amid U.S. export curbs—implies sequential acceleration, with analysts projecting data center sales around $70 billion fueled by Blackwell GPU ramp-up and hyperscaler AI capex surges. markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: Natus Vincere vs Bigetron by Vitality (BO3) - MPL Indonesia Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Natus Vincere and Bigetron by Vitality in the MPL Indonesia Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 10 at 6:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere win the match against Bigetron by Vitality.

This market will resolve to "Bigetron by Vitality" if Bigetron by Vitality win the match against Natus Vincere.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will T1 make a roster change before July?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves T1's active League of Legends roster (defined as the active lineup listed on the T1 Liquipedia page: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/T1) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: Doran, Oner, Faker, Peyz, and Keria. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single match will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be Liquipedia; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no criminal charges against Les Wexner by June 30, reflecting the absence of any active DOJ or FBI prosecution despite longstanding scrutiny over his ties to Jeffrey Epstein. Newly unredacted 2019 FBI files listed Wexner as a potential co-conspirator, prompting a February 2026 House Oversight Committee deposition where he denied knowledge of Epstein's crimes, claiming he was "duped" by his financial manager. Civil lawsuits, including one filed April 6 alleging Wexner funded Epstein's activities, continue, but no indictments have followed congressional inquiries or Attorney General questioning. With no recent grand jury activity or official announcements in the past 30 days, barriers to swift charges remain high absent major new evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Swapped commands a commanding 98.4% implied probability as the top US Netflix movie this week, fueled by its rapid ascent to #1 on daily streaming charts following a blockbuster premiere on May 1. The Skydance Animation family adventure, directed by Nathan Greno and featuring voices like Michael B. Jordan and Juno Temple, racked up 15.5 million views in its first three days—Netflix's strongest animated opening this year—propelled by word-of-mouth buzz, positive audience scores (87% on Rotten Tomatoes), and broad appeal amid light competition. With the weekly chart (Monday-Sunday views) nearing lock on May 11, traders' skin-in-the-game consensus reflects Swapped's sustained momentum; realistic upsets would require an improbable late-week surge from holdovers or viral breakout by a challenger.

markets.question: Will SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12 Superheavy explode?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the booster for the twelfth Starship-SuperHeavy test explodes at any point during the test from the start of fueling operations to 60 minutes after it makes contact with Earth upon landing. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An explosion is defined as a violent and catastrophic event resulting in the destruction of all or part of the vehicle, regardless of intent or context (e.g., a planned termination event would also count).

If the twelfth launch has not occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

Note: The sixth Starship-SuperHeavy test flight which exploded after executing controlled splashdown in the Gulf of Mexico would qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

events.title: SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12
events.description: This is a market on the outcome of the SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX's Starship Flight Test 12, featuring the debut of upgraded V3 ship and Super Heavy booster with 33 Raptor engines, has advanced rapidly following the first full-stack assembly at Starbase's Pad B on May 9. A successful full-thrust static fire on May 7 and prior ship tests signal strong hardware readiness, driving trader consensus toward an imminent launch no earlier than May 15 amid FAA licensing progress. These milestones build on iterative V2 successes, emphasizing rapid reusability and new trajectory profiles from the upgraded pad. Competitive pressures from Blue Origin remain distant, but any static fire anomalies or regulatory holds could delay resolution, underscoring the high-stakes nature of orbital test flights.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Caretaker Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen leads trader consensus at 56.5% implied probability to remain Denmark's next prime minister following the fragmented March 24, 2026 snap election, where her Social Democrats topped the vote but her left bloc fell short of a Folketing majority under proportional representation. Recent coalition negotiations have deadlocked, with the king shifting exploratory talks on May 9 to Liberal Party leader Troels Lund Poulsen (19.1%), now deputy prime minister, boosting his odds amid reports of center-right momentum involving Moderates' Lars Løkke Rasmussen (13.1%), a pivotal kingmaker. Right-wing gains by parties like Danish People's Party add complexity, as no-confidence risks and policy sticking points on immigration and Greenland tensions loom over upcoming rounds.

markets.question: FC Twente '65 vs. Sparta Rotterdam: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game between FC Twente '65 and Sparta Rotterdam, scheduled for May 10 at 10:45 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if FC Twente '65 and Sparta Rotterdam combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Sparta Rotterdam languish in 10th with 12 wins from 30 games, hampered by a recent goalless draw at NAC Breda and a probable defeat to champions PSV on April 11, reflecting mid-table inconsistency away from home. Twente boast a dominant head-to-head record, including a 5-1 thrashing of Sparta in September 2025, bolstered by De Grolsch Veste fortress advantage, though center-back Mees Hilgers remains sidelined with a cruciate ligament tear until late May. Late-season motivation favors the hosts in this round-33 clash.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

Recent gains accelerated on April labor market data previews and reduced US rate cut pricing, though gains appear overstretched ahead of key catalysts including US nonfarm payrolls on May 10, May FOMC minutes, and ECB's June policy review, which could recalibrate market-implied rate paths through year-end.

markets.question: Will the median home value in the DC Metro area be between $566,000 and $572,000 on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in the Washington, D.C. Metro area on May 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for the Washington, D.C. Metro area (Parcl_ID: 2900475). The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 1800 square feet, which is the median home size in the Washington, D.C. Metro area. Parcl is set to publish this data on May 31, 2026. If no data for May 31 is released by June 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/36)

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between LOS and Fluxo W7M in the CBLOL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if LOS and Fluxo W7M play 4 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 4 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: LOS vs Fluxo W7M (BO5) - CBLOL Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between LOS and Fluxo W7M in the CBLOL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "LOS" if LOS win the match against Fluxo W7M.

This market will resolve to "Fluxo W7M" if Fluxo W7M win the match against LOS.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: LØS enters the CBLOL 2026 Split 1 playoffs upper bracket round 1 BO5 against Fluxo W7M with momentum from their 2-1 regular season win over the same opponent on May 3, highlighted by new jungler Curse's debut performance after joining April 30 and shifting Drakehero to substitute. Both squads finished 4-3 in round-robin play—LØS 4th at 9-9 maps, Fluxo 5th at 9-7—with LØS relying on import top Zest, mid Feisty, and support Ackerman alongside bot Duduhh, while Fluxo integrates recent mid cody and support Momochi with jungler Peach. markets.question: Will "Mortal Kombat II" Opening Weekend Box Office be between 35m and 40m?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to how much "Mortal Kombat II" Opening Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its opening weekend. The "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (May 8 - May 10) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend (which typically includes Thursday's previews), regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

If there is no final data available by May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a $35-40 million domestic opening weekend for Mortal Kombat II at 82.5% implied probability, driven by Thursday previews of $5.2 million and a $17 million Friday haul—including previews—that doubled the 2021 original's debut day, though Saturday estimates around $13 million pushed the two-day cume to $30 million, tempering higher pre-release forecasts of $40-50 million. Strong 90% audience scores on Rotten Tomatoes reflect fan enthusiasm for brutal fight scenes and Karl Urban's Johnny Cage, offsetting mixed 70-75% critics' reception labeling it fan-service heavy. Competition from holdover Devil Wears Prada 2 and softer walkups position the $40-45 million bracket at 14.5%, with Sunday's final tally determining resolution amid $80 million budget pressures.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid fragile ceasefire holding after the April 2026 Iran war, U.S.-Iran diplomacy hinges on Tehran's review of a recent one-page U.S. memorandum proposing an end to hostilities, Strait of Hormuz reopening, and nuclear negotiations framework, with no response as of May 8. President Trump urged Iran to sign promptly amid sporadic clashes, while Secretary of State Marco Rubio and envoy Steve Witkoff engaged mediators like Qatar's prime minister on May 9. The prior Islamabad talks on April 10 featured U.S. Vice President JD Vance, Witkoff, and Jared Kushner opposite Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. Traders eye an imminent Iranian reply and potential Geneva venue, which could feature similar mid-level envoys unless escalated.

events.title: WI-05 House Election Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-05 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Rep. Scott Fitzgerald's strong reelection campaign in the solidly Republican WI-05 district, rated R+11 on the Cook Partisan Voting Index, drives trader consensus toward an 84% implied probability for a GOP hold. Fitzgerald, who won 64% in 2024, boasts over $1.4 million cash on hand as of late March, dwarfing Democratic challengers Andrew Beck and Ben Steinhoff's combined under $10,000. No polling exists yet, but forecasters like Cook Political Report rate it Solid Republican. Absent major shifts before the June 1 filing deadline or August 11 primaries, the district's conservative suburban base around Waukesha sustains the lopsided odds, with no notable developments in the past 30 days.

markets.question: Will gas hit (High) $4.60 by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if on any day between market creation and May 31, 2026, the average US regular gas price is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Only the first two decimal digits of the reported price will be considered (e.g., if the price is reported as $3.257, this market will use $3.25 as the price).

The resolution source for this market will be information from the American Automobile Association (AAA), presently found here: https://gasprices.aaa.com/. Specifically, the cell under "Regular" and for the row "Current Avg".

events.title: Will gas hit __ by end of May?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if on any day between market creation and May 31, 2026, the average US regular gas price is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Only the first two decimal digits of the reported price will be considered (e.g., if the price is reported as $3.257, this market will use $3.25 as the price).

The resolution source for this market will be information from the American Automobile Association (AAA), presently found here: https://gasprices.aaa.com/. Specifically, the cell under "Regular" and for the row "Current Avg".

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. national average regular gasoline prices have surged to $4.52 per gallon as of May 10, 2026, per AAA data, up over 7% in the past week amid Middle East tensions disrupting Strait of Hormuz oil flows, refinery outages, and the onset of summer driving season. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude futures hover near $95 per barrel, amplifying retail pump pressures through elevated crack spreads and inventory draws reported in the latest EIA Gasoline and Diesel Fuel Update. Trader sentiment reflects wariness of further supply risks, with seasonal demand peaking around Memorial Day (May 25). Upcoming EIA weekly petroleum status reports and any geopolitical escalations could push prices toward $5, while demand destruction from higher costs offers counterbalance.

markets.question: Will Rayo Vallecano de Madrid vs. Girona FC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 11, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will Morgan Ensberg be the next Red Sox manager?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the person who is appointed as the next permanent manager of the Boston Red Sox.

If no permanent manager is appointed by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other."

Appointments of 'interim,' 'caretaker,' or other non-permanent managers will not impact this market's resolution.

An announcement of a new permanent manager's appointment before this market's close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option, regardless of when the announced appointment goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Boston Red Sox; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Xi meet with Takaichi by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Sanae Takaichi meets with Xi Jinping between market creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Takaichi and Jinping are present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Xi meet with Takaichi by...?
events.description:

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's hawkish stance, including late 2025 remarks on potential military involvement in a Taiwan contingency, continues to strain China-Japan relations six months later, with Beijing maintaining a hardline posture and rejecting Tokyo's recent overtures for a summit at the 2026 APEC gathering. No in-person meeting between Takaichi and President Xi Jinping has occurred since their October 2025 APEC sideline talks, amid ongoing disputes over Senkaku/Diaoyu islands and rare earth exports. Takaichi's February 2026 election victory bolstered her security agenda, prompting alliances with Australia and Vietnam, while trader consensus reflects uncertainty over bilateral diplomacy before year-end, potentially catalyzed by multilateral forums like G20 or APEC.

markets.question: Fraport Skyliners vs. Ratiopharm Ulm

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Fraport Skyliners at a slim 50.5% implied probability for their home BBL matchup against higher-ranked ratiopharm Ulm, capturing the competitive balance from Frankfurt's potent Fraport Arena splits offsetting Ulm's superior fifth-place standing (56.3% win rate) and league-leading defense (102.2 DRTG). Ulm dominated recent head-to-heads, including 115-88 on March 22 and 87-85 on December 23, but Skyliners' home resilience and Ulm's late-season schedule density create even odds. No confirmed injuries or suspensions in the past 48 hours; pre-game injury reports, starting lineups, or momentum shifts from recent form could tip the scales in this playoff-adjacent contest.

markets.question: Will Golden Lions win LRS 2026 Split 1?

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Riot Games (https://lolesports.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Golden Lions dominated the regular season at 6-1 (13-3 games), but Volticons' five-game win streak and head-to-head success in Bo3/Bo5 formats with Fearless Draft have traders pricing a tight race in the double-elimination playoffs. Malvinas Gaming (12%) remains a lower bracket threat pending their May 12 Bo5 against The New Kings, underscoring the bracket's volatility and crowd wisdom on recent form.

events.description: This event is for the upcoming Copa Libertadores game, scheduled for Tuesday, May 19, 2026 between Independiente del Valle and Club Libertad.

eventMetadata.context_description: Club Libertad hosts Independiente del Valle in Copa Libertadores Group H at Estadio Tigo La Huerta, where trader consensus prices reflect a razor-thin contest at 47% apiece for victory and 44% for draw, underscoring balanced strengths amid mutual vulnerabilities. Libertad benefits from home advantage and a recent 3-2 league win but grapples with poor form—no victories in five prior matches—and key absences like ligament-injured Alexis Duarte and Hugo Fernández, plus questionable Hugo Fernández. Independiente del Valle counters with unbeaten away streak (three wins, one draw in last four) yet misses suspended Juan Viacava, replaced by Andy Velasco, after a comeback victory last outing. Head-to-head parity and group stakes fuel the tight dynamics, with both sides prioritizing clean sheets in this pivotal fixture.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former NFL kicker Jay Feely leads trader consensus at 70.5% implied probability to win Arizona's 1st Congressional District Republican primary on July 21, driven by his January Trump endorsement—later clarified as his sole backing after Gina Swoboda shifted races—along with NRCC support and dominant first-quarter fundraising reported in April. State Rep. Joseph Chaplik, who resigned his Scottsdale seat in February to focus on the open contest left by David Schweikert's gubernatorial bid, holds 17.8% amid a residency advantage and an April NextGen poll showing him ahead 24% to Feely's 15%, appealing to voters valuing local legislative experience. A recent televised debate highlighted Feely as a "carpetbagger" for switching from AZ-05, yet skin-in-the-game bets prioritize his national GOP momentum over polling in this competitive primary.

markets.question: Will Lovable be acquired before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if credible reporting confirms that any entity enters into an agreement to acquire the listed company by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 76% implied probability to Cursor's acquisition before 2027, driven by SpaceX's April 21 announcement of a $60 billion option to buy the AI coding assistant—rivaling GitHub Copilot—or pay $10 billion for collaboration, amid surging demand for developer tools in AI infrastructure buildouts. This follows Google's $32 billion Wiz purchase in March, signaling accelerated consolidation in cybersecurity and AI capabilities. Perplexity AI trades at 27% amid Google antitrust pressures bolstering independent AI search plays, while GitLab (24%) and Zoom (17%) reflect bets on DevOps and collaboration software M&A. Key catalysts include SpaceX's summer IPO potentially triggering the Cursor exercise, Q2 earnings for GitLab and Zoom, and regulatory reviews, with agreements—not completions—dictating resolution by year-end.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lagos be 31°C on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ng/lagos/DNMM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
